docs.title: pyscarcopula
docs.text:
Stochastic copula models with Ornstein-Uhlenbeck latent process.

pyscarcopula models time-varying dependence between financial assets. The copula parameter follows a latent stochastic process, estimated via a deterministic transfer matrix method.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Archimedean copulas: Gumbel, Frank, Clayton, Joe (with rotations)
  • Elliptical copulas: Gaussian, Student-t
  • Equicorrelation Gaussian copula: single dynamic correlation for d assets
  • C-vine copulas: automatic family selection, truncation, mixed SCAR/MLE
  • Estimation: MLE, GAS, SCAR-TM-OU (transfer matrix with analytical gradient)
  • Transform functions: xtanh (default), softplus (asymmetric)
  • Diagnostics: GoF test, smoothed parameters

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import GumbelCopula
from pyscarcopula.api import fit, smoothed_params
from pyscarcopula.stattests import gof_test
from pyscarcopula._utils import pobs

u = pobs(returns)
copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180)

result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou')
print(f"logL = {result.log_likelihood:.2f}")

gof = gof_test(copula, u, fit_result=result, to_pobs=False)
print(f"GoF p-value = {gof.pvalue:.4f}")

r_t = smoothed_params(copula, u, result)

docs.title: Comparison on BTC-ETH daily data (T=1460)
docs.text: Model logL GoF p-value MLE (constant) 955.63 0.009 GAS (score-driven) 1031.42 0.528 SCAR-TM 1042.47 0.620

docs.title: Contrib API
docs.text:
Optional modules for risk metrics and marginal models. Not part of the core copula API.

from pyscarcopula.contrib.risk_metrics import risk_metrics
from pyscarcopula.contrib.marginal import MarginalModel

docs.title: pyscarcopula.contrib.risk_metrics.risk_metrics(copula, data, window_len, gamma=0.95, N_mc=100000, marginals_method='johnsonsu', method='scar-tm-ou', optimize_portfolio=True, portfolio_weight=None, n_jobs=1, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Rolling VaR/CVaR estimation with copula models.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default copula BivariateCopula or CVineCopula required data (T, dim) log-returns required window_len int required gamma float or list — confidence level(s) 0.95 N_mc int or list — MC sample sizes 100000 marginals_method str — 'normal', 'johnsonsu', etc. 'johnsonsu' method str — 'mle', 'scar-p-ou', 'scar-m-ou', 'scar-tm-ou' 'scar-tm-ou' optimize_portfolio bool True portfolio_weight (dim,) or None (equal weights) None n_jobs int

Number of parallel workers for rolling window computation. Default 1 (sequential). Use -1 for all CPU cores. Each worker processes a contiguous chunk of windows, so numba compilation overhead is paid once per worker.

1 **kwargs forwarded to copula.fit() {}

Returns:

Name Type Description dict res[gamma][N_mc] = {'var': ..., 'cvar': ..., 'weight': ...}

docs.text: Base class for marginal distribution models.

docs.text: Factory method.

docs.text: Rolling window fit. data: (T, dim). Returns: (T, dim, n_params).

docs.title: ppf(u, params)
docs.text: Inverse CDF. u: (N, dim), params: (dim, n_params). Returns: (N, dim).

docs.title: pyscarcopula.api.fit(copula, data, method='scar-tm-ou', to_pobs=False, config=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Fit a copula to data. Does not mutate the copula.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default copula CopulaProtocol

Stateless copula object.

required data (T, 2) array

Log-returns or pseudo-observations.

required method str

'mle', 'scar-tm-ou', 'scar-p-ou', 'scar-m-ou', 'gas'

'scar-tm-ou' to_pobs bool

Transform data to pseudo-observations first.

False config NumericalConfig or None

Override default numerical constants.

None **kwargs

Forwarded to the strategy's fit() method.

{}

Returns:

Type Description FitResult (immutable dataclass)

docs.title: pyscarcopula.api.smoothed_params(copula, data, result, config=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Time-varying copula parameter.

For MLE: constant array. For SCAR-TM: E[Psi(x_k) | u_{1:k-1}] via transfer matrix. For GAS: Psi(f_t) along filtered path.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default copula CopulaProtocol required data (T, 2) pseudo-observations required result FitResult from fit() required

Returns:

Type Description (T,) array of copula parameters

docs.title: pyscarcopula.api.mixture_h(copula, data, result, config=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
h-function for vine pseudo-observation propagation.

MLE: h(u2, u1; theta_mle) SCAR: E[h(u2, u1; Psi(x)) | data] (mixture over predictive dist) GAS: h(u2, u1; Psi(f_t))

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default copula CopulaProtocol required data (T, 2) pseudo-observations required result FitResult from fit() required

Returns:

Type Description (T,) array

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.base.BivariateCopula
docs.text:
Base class for bivariate copulas (dim=2).

Provides a unified interface for fitting, evaluation, sampling, and diagnostics. Subclasses must override the core density methods; optional overrides enable analytical gradients and batch evaluation.

Subclass contract — must override: pdf_unrotated, log_pdf_unrotated, transform, inv_transform, psi, V

Recommended overrides (enable analytical gradient in TM): dtransform(x) — d Psi / dx dlog_pdf_dr_unrotated(u1, u2, r) — d(log c) / dr

Optional overrides (fused numba batch kernels for speed): pdf_and_grad_on_grid_batch(u, x_grid) copula_grid_batch(u, x_grid)

Estimation methods (via .fit()): 'mle' — constant parameter (1 param) 'scar-tm-ou' — transfer matrix (3 params: theta, mu, nu) 'gas' — GAS score-driven (3 params: omega, alpha, beta) 'scar-p-ou' — MC p-sampler, 'scar-m-ou' — MC m-sampler with EIS

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default rotate int

Copula rotation: 0, 90, 180, or 270 degrees.

0

docs.title: sample(n, r, rng=None)
docs.text: Marshall-Olkin sampling. r: scalar or array (n,). Returns (n, 2).

docs.title: transform(x) staticmethod
docs.text: R -> copula parameter domain.

docs.title: inv_transform(r) staticmethod
docs.text: Copula parameter domain -> R.

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.frank.FrankCopula
docs.text: Frank copula. No rotation support (symmetric).

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.independent.IndependentCopula
docs.text:
Independence copula: C(u1, u2) = u1 * u2.

This is a zero-parameter copula with c(u1, u2) = 1. It serves as the null model for vine edge selection: edges where no parametric copula beats independence by AIC are set to independent, eliminating all fit/forward-pass cost.

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.elliptical.GaussianCopula
docs.text:
d-dimensional Gaussian copula. Parameter: correlation matrix R (d x d).

log c(u; R) = sum_t [ log f_d(x_t; R) - sum_j log f_1(x_{t,j}) ] where x = Phi^{-1}(u).

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.elliptical.StudentCopula
docs.text:
d-dimensional Student-t copula. Parameters: shape matrix R (d x d correlation) and df (degrees of freedom).

log c(u; R, df) = sum_t [ log f_d(x_t; R, df) - sum_j log f_1(x_{t,j}; df) ] where x = t_{df}^{-1}(u).

docs.title: pyscarcopula.stattests.gof_test(model, data, to_pobs=True, seed=None, K=300, grid_range=5.0, fit_result=None)
docs.text:
Unified goodness-of-fit test for any copula model.

Dispatches based on model type: - BivariateCopula -> bivariate Rosenblatt (MLE or SCAR mixture) - CVineCopula -> vine Rosenblatt (per-edge bivariate approach) - GaussianCopula -> Cholesky-based Rosenblatt - StudentCopula -> conditional t-distribution Rosenblatt

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model BivariateCopula, CVineCopula, GaussianCopula, or StudentCopula required data (T, d) array required to_pobs bool True seed int or None None K int — grid size (SCAR only) 300 grid_range float (SCAR only) 5.0 fit_result FitResult or None

If provided, use this instead of model.fit_result. Enables the stateless API: gof_test(copula, u, fit_result=result)

None

Returns:

Type Description CramérVonMisesResult with .statistic and .pvalue

docs.title: pyscarcopula.stattests.vine_gof_test(vine, data, to_pobs=True, seed=None, K=500, grid_range=7.0)
docs.text:
Goodness-of-fit test for a fitted C-vine copula.

Applies the d-dimensional Rosenblatt transform through the vine tree structure, then tests e ~ iid U[0,1]^d via CvM.

For SCAR edges: uses mixture h-function (avoids Jensen bias). For MLE edges: uses constant parameter h-function.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default vine CVineCopula(fitted) required data (T, d) required to_pobs bool True seed int or None None K int — grid size for SCAR mixture Rosenblatt 500 grid_range float 7.0

Returns:

Type Description CramérVonMisesResult with .statistic and .pvalue

docs.title: pyscarcopula.stattests.vine_rosenblatt_transform(vine, u, K=300, grid_range=5.0)
docs.text:
Rosenblatt transform for a fitted C-vine copula.

Each edge in the vine is an independent bivariate copula (possibly with its own latent OU process). The vine Rosenblatt simply applies h-functions level by level, reusing the bivariate approach on every edge — no vine-specific modifications needed.

v[0][i] = u_i v[j+1][i] = h(v[j][i+1] | v[j][0]; edge_{j,i}) e_0 = u_0 e_{j+1} = h(v[j][1] | v[j][0]; edge_{j,0})

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default vine CVineCopula(fitted) required u (T, d) pseudo-observations required K int — grid size for SCAR mixture 300 grid_range float 5.0

Returns:

Name Type Description e (T, d) — should be iid U[0,1]^d under correct model

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.equicorr.EquicorrGaussianCopula
docs.text:
Bases: BivariateCopula

Equicorrelation Gaussian copula for d dimensions.

R(t) = (1-rho(t))I + rho(t)11'

Single scalar parameter rho(t) controls all pairwise correlations. Compatible with SCAR-TM-OU: one latent OU process drives rho(t).

Analytical density: O(d) per evaluation (no matrix operations). Exact d(log c)/drho for analytical gradient.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default d int

Dimension (number of variables). Must be >= 2.

required

docs.title: sample(n, r=None, rng=None)
docs.text: Sample from equicorrelation Gaussian copula.

docs.title: predict(n, rng=None)
docs.text: Sample from stationary OU distribution.

docs.title: smoothed_params(u)
docs.text: Return smoothed rho(t) from TM forward pass.

docs.title: log_likelihood(u, r=None)
docs.text:
Log-likelihood for d-dimensional data.

u : (T, d) pseudo-observations r : float or None — if None, uses MLE rho

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.vine.CVineCopula
docs.text:
C-vine copula for d dimensions.

Decomposes d-dimensional dependence into d(d-1)/2 bivariate copulas arranged in a tree structure. Each edge copula can be from a different family (Gumbel, Clayton, Frank, Joe, Gaussian, or Independent), selected automatically via AIC/BIC.

Tree structure (0-indexed): Tree j, edge i: copula for pair (j+1, j+i+2 | 1..j+1) Tree 0: unconditional pairs (1,2), (1,3), ..., (1,d) Tree 1: conditional pairs (2,3|1), (2,4|1), ... etc.

Estimation supports mixed models: strong edges (Tree 0-1) use SCAR-TM-OU for time-varying parameters, while weak edges (upper trees) fall back to MLE for efficiency. The GoF test handles this correctly via per-edge h-function dispatch.

Key parameters for vine.fit(): method : 'mle', 'scar-tm-ou', 'gas' truncation_level : int — trees >= level stay MLE min_edge_logL : float — edges with MLE logL < threshold stay MLE tol : float — L-BFGS-B tolerance (5e-2 recommended for vine)

Example

vine = CVineCopula(criterion='aic') vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou', min_edge_logL=10) vine.summary() gof = vine_gof_test(vine, u)

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default candidates list of copula classes, or None (default: 5 families) None allow_rotations bool (default True) True criterion 'aic', 'bic', or 'loglik' 'aic'

docs.title: fit(data, method='mle', to_pobs=False, copulas=None, K=300, grid_range=5.0, truncation_level=None, min_edge_logL=None, transform_type='xtanh', **kwargs)
docs.text:
Fit the C-vine copula.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data (T, d) array required method str — 'mle', 'gas', or SCAR method name 'mle' to_pobs bool False copulas None (auto-select) or list-of-lists of 
  (copula_class, rotation) tuples for manual specification.
  copulas[j][i] = (GumbelCopula, 180)
None K int — grid size for SCAR transfer matrix (ignored for MLE/GAS) 300 grid_range float — grid range for SCAR (ignored for MLE/GAS) 5.0 truncation_level int or None

If set, edges at tree levels >= truncation_level keep their MLE fit and are not refitted with SCAR/GAS. This is the standard vine truncation approach (Joe 2014). Example: truncation_level=2 means only trees 0 and 1 use SCAR.

None min_edge_logL float or None

If set, edges whose MLE log-likelihood is below this threshold are not refitted with SCAR/GAS. Weak edges contribute little to the total logL but cost the same to fit. Typical value: 5-10.

None **kwargs forwarded to copula.fit() for SCAR/GAS methods {}

Returns:

Type Description self

docs.title: log_likelihood(data, to_pobs=False, K=300, grid_range=5.0)
docs.text:
Compute total log-likelihood of the C-vine.

For each edge, uses the correct likelihood: MLE: sum log c(u1, u2; theta) GAS: sum log c(u1, u2; Psi(f_t)) SCAR: transfer matrix integrated likelihood

Pseudo-observations for higher trees are computed using the correct h-function (mixture h for SCAR, GAS-filtered h for GAS, constant-parameter h for MLE).

docs.title: sample(n, u_train=None, K=300, grid_range=5.0)
docs.text:
Sample from fitted C-vine using Rosenblatt inverse.

For SCAR: uses mixture_h from u_train (must provide). For GAS: uses GAS-filtered h from u_train. For MLE: u_train not needed.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n int — number of samples required u_train (T, d) or None — training data for dynamic models None K int — grid size for SCAR mixture h 300 grid_range float 5.0

Returns:

Name Type Description x (n, d) — samples in [0,1]^d

docs.title: predict(n)
docs.text:
Predict: sample using x_T from latent process.

For MLE: same as sample (constant params). For SCAR: samples x_T from stationary OU distribution.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n int required

Returns:

Name Type Description x (n, d)

docs.title: summary()
docs.text: Print vine structure summary.

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.vine.VineEdge dataclass
docs.text: One edge in the vine: a fitted bivariate copula.

docs.title: pyscarcopula.copula.vine.select_best_copula(u1, u2, candidates, allow_rotations=True, criterion='aic', transform_type='xtanh')
docs.text:
Select best bivariate copula for (u1, u2) by AIC/BIC/logL.

Always includes IndependentCopula as a baseline competitor. If no parametric copula beats independence, the edge is set to independent (zero cost for SCAR/GAS).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default u1 (T,) arrays required u2 (T,) arrays required candidates list of copula classes required allow_rotations bool True criterion 'aic', 'bic', or 'loglik' 'aic'

Returns:

Name Type Description best_copula fitted BivariateCopula instance best_result fit result

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text: pip install pyscarcopula

docs.title: From source (for development)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/AANovokhatskiy/pyscarcopula
cd pyscarcopula
pip install -e ".[test]"

docs.title: Run tests
docs.text:
pytest tests/

Tests require the data/ directory (included in the git repository but not in the PyPI package).

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Package Min version Purpose numpy 1.22 Arrays, linear algebra numba 0.56 JIT-compiled copula kernels scipy 1.9 Optimization (L-BFGS-B), sparse matrices joblib 1.0 Parallel computation tqdm 4.0 Progress bars

docs.title: Python version
docs.text: Python 3.9 or newer is required. Tested on 3.9–3.13.

docs.title: Prepare data
docs.text:
pyscarcopula works with pseudo-observations — uniform marginals obtained from ranked data.

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pyscarcopula._utils import pobs

prices = pd.read_csv("data/crypto_prices.csv", index_col=0, sep=';')
returns = np.log(prices[['BTC-USD', 'ETH-USD']] /
                 prices[['BTC-USD', 'ETH-USD']].shift(1))[1:].values
u = pobs(returns)

docs.title: Fit a bivariate copula
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import GumbelCopula
from pyscarcopula.api import fit, smoothed_params

copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180)

# Constant parameter (MLE)
result_mle = fit(copula, u, method='mle')

# Time-varying parameter (SCAR)
result_tm = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou')

print(f"MLE:  logL = {result_mle.log_likelihood:.2f}")
print(f"SCAR: logL = {result_tm.log_likelihood:.2f}")

docs.title: Goodness-of-fit test
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.stattests import gof_test

gof = gof_test(copula, u, fit_result=result_tm, to_pobs=False)
print(f"p-value = {gof.pvalue:.4f}")

docs.title: Smoothed copula parameter
docs.text:
r_t = smoothed_params(copula, u, result_tm)
# r_t[k] = E[Psi(x_k) | u_{1:k-1}]

docs.title: Fit a multivariate C-vine
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import CVineCopula

tickers = ['BTC-USD', 'ETH-USD', 'BNB-USD', 'ADA-USD', 'XRP-USD', 'DOGE-USD']
returns_6d = np.log(prices[tickers] / prices[tickers].shift(1))[1:251].values
u6 = pobs(returns_6d)

vine = CVineCopula()
vine.fit(u6, method='scar-tm-ou',
         truncation_level=2, min_edge_logL=10)
vine.summary()

docs.title: Available copula families
docs.text: Family Class Rotations SCAR support Gumbel GumbelCopula 0, 90, 180, 270 Yes Clayton ClaytonCopula 0, 90, 180, 270 Yes Frank FrankCopula 0 Yes Joe JoeCopula 0, 90, 180, 270 Yes Independence IndependentCopula — — Gaussian BivariateGaussianCopula — Yes Equicorrelation EquicorrGaussianCopula — Yes Gaussian (d-dim) GaussianCopula — MLE only Student-t (d-dim) StudentCopula — MLE only

docs.title: Available estimation methods
docs.text: Method Key Description MLE 'mle' Constant copula parameter SCAR-TM-OU 'scar-tm-ou' Transfer matrix with OU latent process GAS 'gas' Observation-driven score model

docs.title: The SCAR model
docs.text:
In the SCAR (Stochastic Copula Autoregressive) model, the copula parameter follows a latent Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process:

\[\theta_t = \Psi(x_t), \qquad dx_t = \theta_\text{OU}(\mu - x_t)\,dt + \nu\,dW_t\]

The three OU parameters control:

  • θ_OU — mean-reversion speed
  • μ — long-run mean of the latent process
  • ν — volatility of the latent process

docs.title: Fitting
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import GumbelCopula
from pyscarcopula.api import fit

copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180)
result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou')

print(result.params.theta, result.params.mu, result.params.nu)
print(result.log_likelihood)

docs.title: Rotations
docs.text:
Rotations capture different tail dependence patterns:

Rotation Tail dependence 0° Upper tail (Gumbel, Joe) or lower tail (Clayton) 90° Mixed 180° Opposite tail 270° Mixed

For financial data (joint crashes), GumbelCopula(rotate=180) or ClaytonCopula(rotate=0) are common choices.

docs.title: Smoothed parameter
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.api import smoothed_params

r_t = smoothed_params(copula, u, result)

Returns the filtered copula parameter at each time step.

docs.title: Goodness of fit
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.stattests import gof_test

gof = gof_test(copula, u, fit_result=result, to_pobs=False)

Uses the Rosenblatt transform with Cramér-von Mises statistic. For SCAR models, integrates the h-function over the predictive distribution (mixture Rosenblatt).

docs.title: Idea
docs.text:
For d assets, the standard Gaussian copula has d(d-1)/2 correlation parameters — all static. The equicorrelation model uses a single dynamic correlation:

\[R(t) = (1-\rho(t)) \cdot I + \rho(t) \cdot \mathbf{1}\mathbf{1}^\top\]

All pairwise correlations equal ρ(t), which follows an OU process via SCAR. This gives 3 parameters instead of d(d-1)/2.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.copula.equicorr import EquicorrGaussianCopula

cop = EquicorrGaussianCopula(d=6)

# MLE (constant rho)
cop.fit(u, method='mle')

# SCAR (time-varying rho)
cop.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou')

docs.title: Goodness of fit
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.stattests import gof_test
gof = gof_test(cop, u, to_pobs=False)

docs.title: Sampling
docs.text:
samples = cop.predict(n=10000)
samples = cop.sample(n=10000, r=0.5)

docs.title: When to use
docs.text:
Equicorrelation SCAR is a good fit when:

  • All pairwise correlations move together (common in equity/crypto markets)
  • You need fast estimation for large d (O(d) per density evaluation)
  • You want a simple interpretable model (3 parameters)

For heterogeneous dependence, use C-vine instead.

docs.title: Bivariate copula
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import GumbelCopula
from pyscarcopula.api import fit

copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180)
result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou')

# Relaxed tolerance (faster)
result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou', tol=5e-2)
Parameter Default Effect analytical_grad True Uses analytical gradient. ~3-4x fewer function evaluations. smart_init True Heuristic initial point. Up to 5x speedup on long series. tol 1e-2 L-BFGS-B gradient tolerance. 5e-2 is ~2x faster with negligible logL loss. K 300 Minimum grid size. May be auto-increased for adequate resolution. pts_per_sigma 2 Grid density. Increase to 4 for very peaked transition kernels. transform_type 'xtanh' 'softplus' often gives better logL on financial data.

docs.title: Vine copula
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import CVineCopula

vine = CVineCopula()
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou',
         truncation_level=2,
         min_edge_logL=10,
         tol=5e-2)
Parameter Default Effect truncation_level None Trees at level >= this stay MLE. Recommended: 2-3 for d > 10. min_edge_logL None Edges with MLE logL below threshold stay MLE. Recommended: 5-10.

d=6 crypto, T=250:

Configuration Time logL GoF p-value Full SCAR (15 edges) ~30s ~928 ~0.90 Truncated SCAR (level=2, logL≥10) ~13s ~922 ~0.90 MLE only ~0.6s ~869 ~0.21

docs.title: NumericalConfig
docs.text:
Override default numerical parameters:

from pyscarcopula._types import NumericalConfig

cfg = NumericalConfig(default_K=500, default_tol_scar=5e-2)
result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou', config=cfg)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The transform function Ψ(x) maps the latent OU process x(t) to the copula parameter domain. For example, Gumbel requires θ ≥ 1, so Ψ: R → [1, ∞).

pyscarcopula provides two transforms:

Name Formula Properties xtanh x·tanh(x) + offset Symmetric, linear growth at ±∞ softplus log(1+eˣ) + offset Asymmetric, floor at offset

docs.title: Choosing a transform
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import GumbelCopula
from pyscarcopula.api import fit

# Default
copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180)

# Softplus (often better on financial data)
copula = GumbelCopula(rotate=180, transform_type='softplus')
result = fit(copula, u, method='scar-tm-ou')

docs.title: softplus advantages
docs.text: The softplus transform has a natural floor — the copula parameter cannot go below a minimum value (e.g., θ=1 for Gumbel, which corresponds to independence). This matches financial data where correlations have a lower bound but can spike arbitrarily high.

docs.title: xtanh advantages
docs.text: Symmetric — treats upward and downward movements equally. May be preferable when the copula parameter can meaningfully decrease below the long-run mean.

docs.title: Using with vine
docs.text:
The transform_type parameter propagates to all edge copulas in a vine:

from pyscarcopula import CVineCopula

vine = CVineCopula()
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou', transform_type='softplus')

docs.title: C-vine structure
docs.text:
A C-vine decomposes a d-dimensional copula into d(d-1)/2 bivariate copulas:

  • Tree 0: unconditional pairs
  • Tree 1+: conditional pairs

Each edge copula is selected automatically from all available families via AIC.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula import CVineCopula
from pyscarcopula._utils import pobs

vine = CVineCopula()
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou',
         truncation_level=2,
         min_edge_logL=10)
vine.summary()

docs.title: Truncation
docs.text:
For large d, not all edges benefit from dynamic parameters:

# Trees 0-1: SCAR, trees 2+: MLE
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou', truncation_level=2)

# Edges with weak MLE dependence stay MLE
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou', min_edge_logL=10)

# Both
vine.fit(u, method='scar-tm-ou',
         truncation_level=2, min_edge_logL=10)

Edges where no parametric copula beats independence by AIC are set to IndependentCopula automatically.

docs.title: Goodness of fit
docs.text:
from pyscarcopula.stattests import gof_test
gof = gof_test(vine, u, to_pobs=False)

The GoF test handles mixed SCAR/MLE vines correctly.

docs.title: Sampling
docs.text:
samples = vine.predict(n=10000)
samples = vine.sample(n=10000, u_train=u)
